title: Working Memory Holds Only a Few Items
script_summary: Exploring the limits of working memory and the power of chunking.

segments.voiceover: Your mental scratchpad is tiny — that's why phone numbers feel hard to keep.
Working memory can only hold about 7 items at once, a limit set by cognitive psychologist George Miller.
So, why do we struggle remembering longer numbers? It’s all about chunking.
Chunking means grouping information into manageable bits, like 555-1234 instead of 5551234.
But did you know? Cowan suggests we can hold even fewer when distractions hit.
Mastering chunking can boost your memory — whether for studying, phone numbers, or daily tasks.
